verified: project-original procedural Hero GT source, deterministic glTF export, official Godot import, and project-owned wrapper
37-node semantic contract including three LODs, collision proxy, wheels, suspension, contacts, cameras, lights, exhausts, materials, and damage zones
visual steering, wheel rotation, measured suspension travel, chase and cockpit cameras, LOD transitions, damage highlights, and graybox equivalence
two-fresh-stage byte-reproducible unsigned Linux release with build inputs excluded
completion_pending: Q-020 project-owner approval of the final art direction and exact Hero GT rights record
excluded_claims: final production silhouette, material richness, cockpit composition, or driving aesthetics
human approval of the provisional project-original CC0 rights declaration

acceptance_comparisons.criterion: the original fictional grand tourer passes source, rights-record, export, import, semantic-node, LOD, collision, and budget contracts
acceptance_comparisons.observed: the deterministic project-original Hero GT passed two rebuilds, three corrupt mutations, clean official import, 37 semantic nodes, three LODs, a 108-triangle collision proxy, and every declared budget; final rights approval remains Q-020

acceptance_comparisons.criterion: the visual rig follows authoritative simulation without owning physics or save state
acceptance_comparisons.observed: the adapter consumes steering, longitudinal speed, and four measured suspension compressions from the existing rigid-body simulation; collision and save ownership are unchanged

acceptance_comparisons.criterion: chase, cockpit, braking, steering, suspension, LOD, damage, and graybox states are deterministic and reviewable
acceptance_comparisons.observed: all eight scenario stages passed; the cockpit stage asserts the declared cockpit camera is current, and the 480-frame renderer capture completed

acceptance_comparisons.criterion: a clean source checkout validates and exports without interactive editor work
acceptance_comparisons.observed: CLI-only Blender and Godot gates passed, the normalized generated scene matched independent import caches, and two fresh release stages produced the same unsigned Linux archive bytes

output_artifacts.retention: local review artifact; the tracked review sheet is durable
local unsigned release artifact; protected CI reproduces platform archives

failure_logs.failure: A sequential release-pack comparison mixed import-cache state and could not support a reproducibility claim.
failure_logs.recovery: The authoritative release gate now compares two entirely fresh source stages.

failure_logs.failure: Fresh official Godot imports assigned different non-semantic node unique IDs to otherwise identical scene content.
failure_logs.recovery: The promotion gate converts the official import to text and strips only numeric node unique_id attributes before tracking the generated scene.

failure_logs.failure: Export-time text-to-binary conversion regenerated cache-specific scene IDs and changed the PCK.
failure_logs.recovery: The project ships native deterministic text scenes; two fresh complete releases now match byte for byte.

failure_logs.failure: The Linux-only release completed successfully but returned the unused Windows predicate as exit status 1.
failure_logs.recovery: Explicit platform conditionals now preserve a successful single-platform exit status.

failure_logs.failure: The night cockpit review approximated the view with the chase camera instead of exercising the semantic cockpit anchor.
failure_logs.recovery: The stage now switches to the cockpit-anchor camera and asserts it is current.

recovery_result: All autonomous technical, runtime, renderer, regression, asset-pipeline, and reproducible-release gates pass at the recorded revision; only the explicit human art-direction and rights approval remains open.

human_gate.name: Hero vehicle art direction and final rights approval
